name: 16p11_2_BOLA2_BP4_BP5_duplicons
description: BOLA2/SLX1/SULT1A duplicon family forming BP4 and BP5 of the 16p11.2 proximal recurrent CNV. The BOLA2 duplicon is human-specific (~282 kya, Nuttle 2016) and its tandem copy expansion created the NAHR substrate that drives 16p11.2 microdeletion/microduplication.

architecture.internal_structure: BP4 and BP5 each contain BOLA2A/B, SLX1A/B, SULT1A3/A4 paralogs at ~99.5% identity over ~102 kb. BOLA2 copy number varies 3-8 in humans; absent in Neanderthals/Denisovans. Plays mechanistic role in iron-sulfur cluster biogenesis.
